observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Trace the unreconciled balance of ₱3,176,570.92 in the Net Operating Cashflows and restate line items of the Statement of Cash Flows as deemed necessary; and
Ensure that reconciling items are consistent with accounts related to operating activities in other financial statements to establish accuracy of Net Operating Cash Flows presented in the Statement of Cash Flows.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Adjust the following affected accounts using the following entry:
Discontinue the policy of assigning residual value to the LRNs and modify the computation of the depreciation expense by disregarding the residual value in the formula.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to comply with the disclosure requirements and ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust and provide accordingly the depreciation expense of the infrastructure assets and buildings accounts to present fairly the carrying amount of the said assets in the books of accounts of the municipality by taking up the following suggested journal entry:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MA to immediately de-recognize the PPE erroneously recorded in the books using the following journal entries:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Municipal Assessor to re-evaluate the actual use of the portions of leased municipality-owned properties to private entities and update the tax declarations, as applicable; and
The Municipal Treasurer to enforce the collection of RPT against the private entities leasing a portion of the properties of the municipality.
We also recommended that, for the renewal of the lease contracts of portions involving the People’s Center, consider removing the provision that waives the payment of RPT by the lessee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
Enforce strict compliance of the contractors by ensuring that project billboards, as a line item in the POWs, are undertaken by the contractors in order to achieve full disclosure in the implementation of government projects and the right of the people to access up-to-date information on matters of public concern; and
Stop certifying an infrastructure project as accomplished, if the project billboard was not undertaken by the contractors so that the same shall not be paid.
We further recommended that Management promptly inform both the Supervising Auditor and the Audit Team Leader within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards are already posted, thereby facilitating the validation of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
Strictly require all contractors to ensure their entire workforce is wearing the required safety gear or personal protective equipment at all times within the construction project site for the protection and welfare of both the construction workers and the general public;
Closely monitor the proper implementation of the CSHP and standards by the contractors at the project sites by maintaining detailed records of contractor’s non-compliance per site visitation, which can be referenced during future payment processes; and
Formulate an internal policy that clearly defines the appropriate sanctions or penalties to be applied to contractors found in violation of the CSHP to ensure accountability and adherence to safety protocols.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Department Heads to identify the projects, programs and activities in their respective departments that require meals and snacks; and
The BAC to consolidate the requirement of all the departments and procure the meals and snacks of the municipality through competitive bidding adopting the Framework Agreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource and Management Officer to prepare list of positions in its plantilla with the corresponding job description or actual duties that describes the basis for justifying such positions for those who are exposed to occupational risks, and establish a procedure for identifying personnel who shall be exposed to hazardous and occupational risks, including the duration of exposure.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant that claims for hazard pay for Social Workers must be supported with proof of actual service rendered during the period being claimed and that the same be computed in accordance with the rules and regulations; and
Further, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO I and SWO I to refund the overpayment amounting to ₱33,569.70 broken down as follows:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO to ensure, during the inspection of delivered items, that the medicines have a minimum shelf life of 18 months as prescribed by the AO.
Likewise, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to include the following:
In the bidding documents, under Section V on Special Conditions of Contract, the delivery requirements, such as the minimum expiration date, and ensure that the delivery receipt includes the lot number and expiration date of the items; and
A provision requiring the supplier to provide a certification stating that any remaining items will be replaced before they expire if the supplier is unable to deliver medicines with the specified shelf life or delivers products with a shorter shelf life than prescribed by the Administrative Order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO and the personnel in the Municipal Health Office to coordinate during inspection, ensuring that the delivered medicines comply with FDA approval and certification.
Furthermore, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to include a requirement in the bidding documents that all delivered medicines, must be registered with the FDA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department heads to keep updated on the latest issuances of guidelines, rules and regulations especially those affecting the operations of the municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Development Council to utilize the 20% DF to finance the priority development projects of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanism of the Municipality’s CBMS and GAD Databases through preparation of master plan indicating the timelines, process, budget and other necessary actions. Thereafter, start the implementation in CY 2021 in order to ensure updated, operational and reliable information provided by said databases which will serve the Municipal Government in attaining an evidence-based planning, policy-making, and relief distribution in the future.

Additionally, we have recommended that Management augment the number of employees responsible for the maintenance and development of the databases, more specifically to update the data contained therein as data gathering and collating mechanism of the GAD and CBMS Databases is certainly a tedious work.

Lastly, we have recommended that Management coordinate with data-gathering government agencies the Municipal’s need for information related to update of databases.  And if necessary, perhaps the Municipality could enter into a partnership or an agreement with such agency, to hasten the retrieval or gathering of data and information.

We have recommended that the Management prospectively assert the rights emanating from the Memorandum of Agreement which was signed and entered into by both parties.

We also recommended that the Management tap additional suppliers so that in the event of insufficiency of supplies, relief operations of the Municipality will not be hampered.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Engineer to furnish copies of GPPA Report to the Municipal Accountant and to include completed and on-going projects in the financial statements.

In relation hereto, the Municipal Accountant adjust the PPE accounts recognizing additional PPE in CY 2020 amounting to P23,113,687.94 and recognizing corresponding depreciation.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Property and Accounting Units to investigate, correct, adjust and record the unreconciled PPE balances between the books and inventory report and undertake the procedural guidelines enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to come up with more reliable account balances to be presented in the financial statements.

We also recommended that Management expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties to free up storage spaces, prevent further deterioration of PPE and generate additional income from sale.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS by adequately providing a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of material balances to ensure that relevant information are available to the intended users of the Financial Statements.

We have therefore recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to a) restate the Statement of Financial Performance by making necessary correcting entries for OMOE and related accounts and b) use asset method in recording inventories in compliance with the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct:

the Municipal Treasurer to follow-up with the installation of Land Bank of The Philippines Automated Teller Machine within the Agoo Farmers’ Multi-Purpose Building; and
the Human Resources Management Officer (HRMO) to continue the enrollment of personnel for LBP automated Payroll Clearing System so that once the ATM is installed, the LGU can start the using it and reduce the cash accountability of the DO which would eventually reduce or mitigate the risk of loss or misapplication of government funds.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the BAC-TWG to conduct validation and checking of the statement of ongoing government and private contracts during the post qualification to have an assurance that the bidders have satisfactory performance in their government or private contracts.  Moreover, in doing so, the capacity of the bidders to perform the scope of works on their ongoing and upcoming projects will not be compromised resulting to suspensions, delays and deficiency.

Based on the foregoing, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the:
MSWDO to review/re-evaluate the existing policies and procedures on AICS Program, and adopt a written policy on the determination as to the appropriateness, procedure and payment of financial assistance to individuals/families in crisis situation, considering the nature, eligibility of beneficiaries, amount of financial assistance and such other measures that would facilitate the effective and efficient implementation of the AICS Program of the Municipality; and
the Municipal Treasurer, with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan, to establish petty cash fund, solely for financial assistance, in order to ensure effectiveness and efficiency of AICS program.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold taxes on payments of BAC honoraria and remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) in conformity with National Internal Revenue Code of 1997 and avoid sanctions on non-performance thereof.

We therefore recommended the Management identify pre-determined or target outputs or outcomes of the project and make an assessment of the actual accomplishments so that informative decisions may be made for similar contracts that the Municipality plans to enter into.

Additionally, we recommended that Management attempt to strike a better deal with MAWI, as it is presently the least affordable water servicing company in the province.  Perhaps, MAWI could decrease the fees collected from the customer, or maybe they could waive the minimum water servicing fees being charged for the various public water connections, such as the fire hydrants.

Finally, we also recommended the Management to identify, and quantify if possible, the goals and objectives of a project so that assessment in the future may be made in the presence of relevant and comparable data.

We have therefore recommended that Management:

make an assessment of the actual accomplishments of the project so that informative decisions may be made for similar contracts that the Municipality plans to enter into;
conduct reassessment of the profile of the private corporation in order to ensure that the Filipino First Policy is upheld; and
ascertain that the provisions in the agreement are in specific in its terms and all-encompassing, ultimately to protect the interest of the Local Government Unit.

We have recommended that Management ensure proper presentation of the budget consistent with the Revised Chart of Accounts provided under Department of Budget and Management Local Budget Circular No. 77 so that corresponding utilization be charged to proper appropriation and consequently, present fairly the financial statements of the Municipality which will aid in the sound decision-making of the Management and other stakeholders.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local
Chief Executive:
a. Create an Inventory Committee, thereafter, direct the said committee to
conduct physical inventory of all existing properties of the Municipality
and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) and
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) on or before the deadline as required under Section 124 of
Manual on NGAS; and
b. Require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed
records of property records, such as stock cards; and property cards and
property, plant and equipment ledger cards and real property ledger
cards, respectively. Reconcile the said records with the RPCI and
RPCPPE pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the above-cited manual.

Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the Management agreed to create an Inventory
Committee. The Municipal Mayor further instructed the GSO-Designate to conduct
the physical inventory, to maintain the prescribed forms of property records, and to
reconcile with the records of the Municipal Accountant. Reports of the Physical
Count will be submitted within the deadline.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a. Direct the GSO-Designate to document the application for disposal
of unserviceable properties to the COA Auditor. The application
should be supported by the prescribed forms pursuant to the
Handbook on Property and Supply Management System and
Section 79 of PD No. 1445; and
b. Create a Disposal Committee.

Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the Management agreed to create a Disposal
Committee, including the Municipal Engineer who will conduct the appraisal of the
unserviceable properties. The GSO-Designate agreed to prepare the Report of
Unserviceable Properties to support the disposal of such properties.

We have recommended and Management agreed that for future similar
transactions, the Municipal Accountant will use the proper account classification
in the recognition of transactions in the books of account in order to reflect the
correct balances of each account in the financial statements. We have also
recommended that the list of beneficiaries with corresponding quantities issued,
address and signatures be submitted immediately in order for us to validate the
said distribution.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), as Head of
the Local School Board (LSB), comply with the existing laws, rules and
regulations in the utilization of additional one percent (1%) Special Education
Tax so that the fund will be optimally used for the purposes cited above.

We have further recommended that the amount be reverted back to the
Special Education Fund and the amount of P241,615.19 be charged to the
General Fund (GF).

Management Comment:
Management believes that all expenses related to education are chargeable
against SEF.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team affirms our stand that charging of expenses against SEF should
be limited only to those Programs, Projects and Activities (PAPs) as provided by
Sections 100(c) and 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 4.0 of
Department of Education-Department of Budget and Management-Department of
Interior and Local Government Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We have recommended that Management enter into a Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) with the school beneficiaries through the school principal in
order to have a basis in compeling them to prepare and submit the required
narrative reports and photo documentation. We further recommended that in
the said MOA, a provision must be incorporated requiring the school principal to
immediately inform the LGU’s focal person in cases there are recipient/s who
had dropped out of school during the period of the project implementation.
Lastly, we have recommended that Management submit the complete proof
of receipt by the scholars and photo documentation immediately for validation
and to support the liquidation report of these funds.

Management Comment:
Management informed the Audit Team that there was a Memorandum of
Agreement between the LGU and the Department of Education (DepEd) with regard
to the said program. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) also instructed the concerned
LGU Officials to evaluate whether the bi-monthly report on accomplishments be
changed into a monthly report.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the drivers
concerned to accomplish properly the Driver’s Trip Tickets to comply with the
afore-cited regulation for proper control and monitoring of fuel expenses.
Further, for Management to adopt a sound internal control measures,
procedures and guidelines in the use of fuel to avoid misuse and wastage of
government resources.

Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the Management justified that the garbage trucks
were not only used in collecting garbage around the Municipality but also used for
other projects hence, there were times where re-fueling is more often. They also
informed the Audit Team that they already started to use a logbook in 2019 for proper
monitoring of the fuel consumption. A meeting between the Management and with
the drivers will also be conducted informing them on the proper filling-up of the
Driver’s Trip Ticket/s. For those municipal vehicles with defective kilometer reading,
it was agreed upon that the driver/s will indicate “Not Functional” in the Driver’s Trip
Ticket/s.

We have recommended that the materials needed for the backfilling of the
new cemetery be indicated in the procurement documents in conformity with the
provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 so that the reasonableness and the
competitiveness of the prices being availed of by the LGU be ascertained and that
the desired quality of the project be fully achieved.

Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer admitted that
indicating the type of backfilling materials was overlooked in the Program of Works
(POW). However, to ensure a better output, compaction was already done. He
assured the Audit Team that in future similar projects, the type of materials to be used
will be specified in the POW.
Auditor’s rejoinder:
We have stressed during our exit conference that the Municipal Engineer
immediately submit the Program of Work incorporating the materials needed in the
backfilling of the new cemetery in order for us to reasonably compute the correctness
of the amount paid to the contractor.

We have recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Engineer require the contractor to accomplish the unfinished item of work
otherwise the latter must refund the equivalent amount. We also have
recommended Management that liquidated damages be imposed equal to at
least one tenth (1/10) of one (1) percent of the cost of the unperformed
portion of the works for every day of delay but not to exceed 10% of the
amount of contract.

Management comment:
During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer justified that there was
a change order on the project however, they failed to furnish a copy for the
Commission on Audit.

We have recommended that Local Chief Executive (LCE), as Head of
the Procuring Entity, ensure that prior to contract signing for infrastructure
projects, require the winning bidder to submit CSHP approved by the DOLE
which forms part of the contract in conformity with Section 37.2.3 of the
Republic Act No. 9184 in order to promote the welfare of the construction
workers.

Management Comment:
In reply to our Audit Observation Memorandum, the Management
informed the Audit Team that the Municipality of Agoo, La Union is
implementing Section 37.2.3(f) of RA 9184. Likewise, Management required the
contractors to submit the approved CSHP, however, the contractors cannot submit
on the desired time due to some delays. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
had formulated a plan to improve the process to have a smooth flow of documents
to meet the desired time as schedule on the procurement process. In addition, an
Executive Order was furnished to the Audit Team authorizing the Municipal
Mayor the Municipal Administrator to sign documents needed in the application
for the said CSHP and the Letter to contractors.

We have recommended that the LGU, through its Municipal
Engineer, formulate plans, programs, techniques, procedures and practices
in infrastructure development and public works in general to promote
maximum efficiency of public resources. Immediate measures should be done
to apply proper water proofing materials in order to avoid further damages
and deterioration of the building.

Management Comment:
The Management thru the Municipal Engineer, informed the Audit Team
that the contractor is willing to shoulder the additional expense for the repair of
the damages on the said project. In addition, the Municipal Engineer informed
both the Audit Team and other Municipal Officials that Phase III will be
implemented by Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) and shall
cover the second level. Thru the implementation of Phase II and Phase III, it is
expected that the leaks would soon be eradicated.

We have recommended that Management ensure and monitor the full
implementation of the projects/programs/activities appropriated under the
20% Development Fund in order to address the needs of the constituents
such as access to improved health, social, agriculture and social services.
Further, in order to address the needs of the constituents on a timely
manner, improve the preparation of Annual Investment Program and
Annual Procurement Plan by providing timelines.

Management Comment:
In reply to our Audit Observation Memorandum issued, the Management
enumerated the status of the programs, projects and activities of the Municipality:
1. The Construction of Ube Processing Center amounting to
P1,000,000.00; Construction of PC Pavement at UB Processing Center
amounting to P800,000.00; and Construction of Wastewater Treatment
Facility at Municipal Slaughterhouse amounting to P1,000,000.00 were
completed on October 29, 2018, February 26, 2019 and November 13,
2018 respectively, but the final payments were still in process;
2. Construction of Agoo Farmer’s Multi-Purpose Building (Phase III)
amounting to P4,300,000.00 was completed on February 28, 2019 while
the Phase II from the continuing appropriation amounting to
P9,725,768.33 was completed on February 18, 2019 and both 80% of it
were disbursed and still on process for the final payments;
3. Rewiring of Municipal Hall/Provision of Fire Exit amounting to
P500,000.00 is still on-going with suspension order;
4. Expansion/Improvement of Gonzales St. with an ABC of P1,220,000.00,
P1,089,344.03 was disbursed and already fully paid;
5. Construction/Installation of Disaster/Warning Signs and Street
Directional Signs amounting to P500,000.00 is still on-going;
6. Installation of Artificial Reef worth P1,500,620.80 was already fully
paid;
7. Procurement of Garbage Compactor amounting to P3,142,000.00 from
the continuing appropriation, only P1,672,000.00 was disbursed while
the remaining P1,470,000.00 was charged to Trust Fund and realigned
for Construction of Children’s Park with Playground and Accessories at
Eco-Fun World and scheduled for public bidding on March 18, 2019;
8. Construction of Road with RC Canal at Verceles St. (Phase I) amounting
to P2,946,141.19 from the continuing appropriation was already fully
paid;
9. Construction of Wastewater Treatment Facility at Municipal
Slaughterhouse amounting to P477,787.00 from the continuing
appropriation was completed on November 13, 2018 and the final
payment is still in process;
10. Improvement of Eco-Fun World (Phase I) amounting to
P4,430,000.00 from the current appropriation and P360,000.00 from the
continuing appropriation are scheduled for public bidding on March 19,
2019.

We have recommended that the Municipal Officials and Employees
fully implement projects/programs/activities in accordance with DILG-
approved GAD Plan and Budget to fully address gender issues and women
empowerment of the constituents. We have further recommended that the
GAD Focal Point System ensure and monitor strict implementation of the
annual GAD PPAs for the constituents to fully utilize the benefit that could
be derived therefrom.

Management Comment:
Management agreed to the recommendation and ensured the Audit Team
that they would monitor the implementation of the annual GAD PPAs for the
succeeding years.

We have recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to submit
the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and corresponding Accomplishment
Report within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review of the same.

We have recommended that Management, along with the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC), monitor and cease the practice of indicating the
brand names in the Purchase Requests and Purchase Orders, as well as in
the Request for Quotations submitted by the suppliers, in order to comply
with Section 18 of RA 9184. Rather, use a generic description of the product
and that reference to brand names should only be allowed for items or parts
that are compatible with the existing fleet or equipment of the same make.

Management Comment:
The Management agreed to the audit recommendation and will conduct a
meeting with the Department Heads to avoid the indication of brand names in the
Purchase Request, Purchase Order, and Request for Quotations.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to comply with
the above-mentioned provisions so that the Audit Team will be able to
conduct timely evaluation/review of items purchased and any
defects/deficiencies noted thereon will be immediately communicated to the
Management.

We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer the following:
 Continue to demand settlement of tax liabilities from the identified
taxpayers;
 Document the main reasons why these remained uncollected for
the past years;
 Adopt new strategies to address the reason/s for non-payment;
and
 In coordination with the Provincial Treasury Office, avail of the
administrative and legal remedies provided by law to enforce
collection of the delinquent real property taxes.

Management Comment:
During the exit conference, Management agreed to the recommendation
and will create a team, composed of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, Office
of the Municipal Assessor, and Office of the Municipal Engineer, to conduct a
house-to-house visit on the delinquent taxpayers.

We have recommended and the Management agreed to see to it that
all the required supporting documents be attached to the disbursement
vouchers before payment be made to avoid disallowance or suspension in the
future. We further recommended to management enforce the immediate
settlement of all disallowances by the person’s held liable under the notices of
disallowances.

We have recommended that Management comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 97-002 and refrain the use of Petty Cash Fund for the payment of regular expenditures like the financial assistance, which could be paid out of other regular disbursement process like authorizing a disbursing officer to draw cash advance for that purpose.
observations.managementAction: Payment was made on regular disbursement process.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management stop granting cash advances to officer/employee that is not an official or employee of the municipality. We have further recommended that the certification required above be submitted to substantiate the validity of her claim to the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management admitted their lapses and adopted some control measure/s with regard to cash advances, particularly to those other than an official or employee of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to comply with the regulations cited above so as not to affect the benefits accruing to the officials and employees of the municipality. In doing so, penalties, if any, that may be imposed on the employer, will be avoided.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the reconciliation for the Due to GSIS account and part of the discrepancies were already remitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management discontinue allowing officers and employees of the municipality to avail monetization if this would result to having a balance of less than five (5) days of vacation leave to ensure compliance with the provisions of CSC Resolution No. 98-3142 (the Omnibus Rules on Leave) dated December 14, 1998 in the future monetization of leave credits.
observations.managementAction: The Management admitted their lapses and had already adopted control measure/s to monitor those who are entitled for such benefit.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management limit the practice of serving meals and snacks to various visitors of the municipality and observe prudence in utilizing government funds. In doing so, additional funds could have been used for programs and projects to promote the general welfare of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management assured the Audit Team that such transactions on meals and snacks were made for an official purpose, as proven by the attached program of activities and Inspection and Acceptance Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Management to transfer to the General Fund the 2012 unutilized balance of LDRRMF amounting to P1,757,650.77 as mandated under the above regulation in doing so, additional funds could be used for social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: The 2012 unutilized balance of LDRRMF amounting to P1,757,650.77 was already transferred to the General Fund in May, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to prioritize the establishment of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation as required under the above regulation.
observations.managementAction: had already established a GAD database in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Management to comply with the provisions of Section 338 of R.A. 7160 and Section 88 of P.D. 1445 on the prohibition of granting advance payment for services, materials and goods not yet rendered or delivered. We have further recommended to the Local Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that principal/supplemental receipts/invoices accepted are in compliance with BIR Revenue Regulations No. 18-2012.
observations.managementAction: The Management acknowledges such observation and assured the Audit Team that payments related to the procurement of goods/services, particularly socio-economic activities, shall be made after goods were completely delivered or services were satisfactorily rendered.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management strictly comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001. In addition, the management is required to submit the lacking documents noted to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) instructed its Technical Working Group (TWG) to utilize the contract review and analysis sheet and to immediately determine the lacking documents and require the contractor to comply. Though there were still some lacking documents for some infrastructure projects, as required in the Inspection Report prepared by the COA-TAS, the Municipality was able to comply with the lacking documents within the reasonable period.
observations.reason: Though there were still some lacking documents for some infrastructure projects, as required in the Inspection Report prepared by the COA-TAS, the Municipality was able to comply with the lacking documents within the reasonable period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to oblige the Municipal Engineer to ensure compliance with the quality standards in the preparation of plans and specifications in the construction of FMR to promote road safety to all road users and to prolong the useful lives of the FMR.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer assured that the design plans and specifications of farm-to-market road (FMR) projects will be in accordance with rules and regulations of DPWH and other related agencies.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to supervise and monitor collections of stall rentals particularly those located in the Agoo Civic Center and Imelda Garden. The Municipal Treasurer send demand letters to those delinquent stall owners so that additional funds could be used to finance projects, programs that would benefit its constituents. We have further recommended to the Municipal Accountant that the receivables be taken up in the books of the agency so as not to understate the asset and income accounts of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer attested that demand letters were sent and had a dialogue with the delinquent stall owners, thus full settlement was made in the 2018. While, the Municipality Accountant requested that a copy of Municipality collectibles as of end of the year be provided to her office as a basis in recording in the books of accounts. Receipt of stall rentals were directly credited to the income account.
observations.reason: However, the Municipality does not recognize stall receivable in its books.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to assess the causes of delays in the project implementation and address all issues accordingly in order to fast track the implementation of priority development projects under the calendar year 2016 Annual Investment Program inorder that constituents could benefit from the projects. We have recommended further that management allocate the 20% Development Fund to programs/projects outlined under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-11 to ensure that public funds are spent judiciously and only for the purpose for which such funds are intended.
observations.managementAction: Management to implement the Programs based on the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 13 of this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the management agreed to comply with the above-mentioned provisions so that the Audit team will be able to conduct timely evaluation/review of items purchased and any defects/ deficiencies noted thereon will be immediately communicated to the management.
observations.managementAction: Management to submit the documents as required under Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 10 of this audit report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the management the following:
Conduct physical inventory of all existing properties of the municipality as required under Section 124 of NGAS Manual;
Submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor’s office not later than January 31 of each year; and
Maintain property cards duly reconciled with the accounting records pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the same manual.

We have recommended and the management agreed to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 97-002 and refrain the use of Petty Cash Fund for the payment of regular expenditures like the financial assistance, which could be paid out of other regular disbursement process like authorizing a disbursing officer to draw cash advance for that purpose.

We have recommended and the management agreed to stop granting cash advances to officer/employee that is not an official or employee of the municipality.  We have further recommended that the certification required above be submitted to substantiate the validity of her claim to the municipality.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to comply with the regulations cited above so as not to affect the benefits accruing to the officials and employees of the municipality.  In doing so, penalties, if any, that may be imposed on the employer, will be avoided.

We have recommended and the management agreed to discontinue allowing officers and employees of the municipality to avail monetization if this would result to having a balance of less than five (5) days of vacation leave to ensure compliance with the provisions of CSC Resolution No. 98-3142 (the Omnibus Rules on Leave) dated December 14, 1998 in the future monetization of leave credits.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash fund transfers and to institute action against NGO/PO’s and their officers/organizers that failed to liquidate the fund transfer  pursuant to the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement covering these transactions.

We have recommended and the management agreed to limit the practice of serving meals and snacks to various visitors of the municipality and observe prudence in utilizing government funds.  In doing so, additional funds could have been used for programs and projects to promote the general welfare of the constituents.

We have recommended and the management agreed to transfer to the General Fund the 2012 unutilized balance of LDRRMF amounting to P1,757,650.77 as mandated under the above regulation in doing so, additional funds could be used for social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to prioritize the establishment of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation as required under the above regulation.

We have recommended and the management agreed to comply with the above-mentioned provisions so that the Audit team will be able to conduct timely evaluation/review of items purchased and any defects/deficiencies noted thereon will be immediately communicated to the management.

We have recommended and management agreed to comply with the provisions of Section 338 of R.A. 7160 and Section 88 of P.D. 1445 on the prohibition of granting advance payment for services, materials and goods not yet rendered or delivered.  We have further recommended to the Local Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that principal/supplemental receipts/invoices accepted are in compliance with BIR Revenue Regulations No. 18-2012.

It is recommended that management strictly comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  In addition, the management is required to submit the lacking documents noted to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement of infrastructure projects.
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to implement the priority programs and projects which remained unimplemented inorder to uplift the lives of its constituents.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to oblige the Municipal Engineer ensure compliance with the quality design standards in the preparation of plans and specifications in the construction of FMR to promote road safety to all road users and to prolong the useful lives of the FMR.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to supervise and monitor collections of stall rentals particularly those located in the Agoo Civic Center and Imelda Garden.  The Municipal Treasurer send demand letters to those delinquent stall owner so that additional funds could be used to finance projects, programs that would benefit its constituents.
We have further recommended to the Municipal Accountant that the receivables be taken up in the books of the agency so as not to understate the asset and income accounts of the municipality.

We have recommended and the management agreed to see to it that all the required supporting documents be attached to the disbursement vouchers before payment be made to avoid disallowance or suspension in the future.  We further recommended to management enforce the immediate settlement of all disallowances by the person’s held liable under the notices of disallowances.

We have recommended and the management agreed to prepare an adjusting entry to take up the transfer of said properties to the books of the barangays inorder to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.  Consequently, require the Barangay Record Keeper to take up said transfer to the books of accounts of the barangays.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 1 of this audit report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the MSWDO compliance to the guideline in procuring iron fortified rice and to submit the quarterly nutritional status of children and post evaluation to determine the effectiveness of the Supplemental Feeding Program. We also recommended immediate implementation of the program after receipt of the fund transfer from the DSWD. Further, the Municipal Accountant to take up the fund transfer as Due to National Government Agencies account in the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: As per MSWDO, they are conducting monthly monitoring and quarterly measuring of day care children. Iron fortified rice was not procured because the children will not eat them. Further, the Municipal Accountant has already reclassified the fund transfer as Due to NGA under Trust Fund account last August 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to revise the plan following the format prescribed by the NSWMC inorder to have a concrete guideline in the implementation of Solid Waste Management.
observations.managementAction: Updated the 10-year Solid Waste Management Program (SWMP) in accordance with the prescribed form of the NSWMC to be submitted to the DENR Regional Office I.
